items.title: CPO prices to range between RM4,400 and RM4,650 in July on El Nino risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crude palm oil prices are expected to range between RM4,400 and RM4,650 per tonne in July, supported by a tighter supply outlook in Indonesia and growing concerns over El Niño risks, the Malaysian Palm Oil Council (MPOC) said. MPOC said that while oil palm plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia have not been impacted as of June, the possibility of a stronger El Niño from July or August is increasing. Malaysia's palm oil output fell by 6.9 per cent month-on-month to 1.51 million tonnes in May, as oil palm trees entered a temporary resting period after recording above-average production levels between October 2025 and March 2026.

items.title: JPA offers 640 sponsorship slots for outstanding SPM leavers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Public Services Department (PSD) is offering 640 sponsorships to outstanding Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) leavers under its continuous effort to strengthen national talent development. For this year, 30 places are offered under the National Sponsorship Programme and 200 slots under the SPM Leavers Special Programme. A total of 140 slots are allocated for the Special Programme for Japan, Korea, France and Germany (JKPJ), while the JPA-Mara Special Programme (PKJM) involves 270 slots.

items.title: Malaysia's competitiveness ranking surge signals stronger economic fundamentals: Economists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's improved standing in the International Institute for Management Development (IMD) 2026 World Competitiveness Ranking reflects the country's strengthening economic fundamentals. This has been supported by robust trade competitiveness, gains in government and business efficiency, as well as growing opportunities in the technology sector, economists said. Juwai IQI global chief economist Shan Saeed described Malaysia's improved ranking as one of the most remarkable competitiveness gains among major economies globally.

items.title: Top Glove sees softer Q4 sales amid lower ASP, analysts say
items.summary: This comes amid declining average selling prices (ASP) and expectations that prices could fall further before buyers begin replenishing their inventories, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said. HLIB noted that NBR prices have continued to decline, while natural rubber prices have increased 6.3 per cent quarter-to-date. CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd said Top Glove's ASP reached its peak in May 2026 before beginning to normalise in June 2026 as raw material costs eased.

items.title: BTH: Johor's free-for-all contest & BN-PH face-off [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor state election is more than just a contest for 56 seats in the state assembly, it is shaping up to be one of Malaysia's most closely watched political tests, with implications that could extend far beyond the state. In this week's episode, we look into the state's constituencies that are expected to be fiercely contested, making the election a barometer of voter sentiment ahead of the next general election. Political analyst Professor Dr Azizuddin Sani joined the set.

items.title: Cabinet approves National Tahfiz Council, Zahid to chair new body
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 19 — The federal government has approved the establishment of the National Tahfiz Council, marking a major step in streamlining the direction, education model, recognition standards and academic pathways for tahfiz students nationwide. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approval was given at last week’s Cabinet meeting. Meanwhile, the Deputy Prime Minister expressed hope that the establishment of the National Tahfiz Council would position tahfiz education as a recognised educational pathway within the education system.

items.title: Father, son injured in Klang house fire
items.summary: KLANG: A father and son were injured after a fire destroyed 70 per cent of their double-storey terraced house in Taman Seri Istana Hills here early this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call at 5.23am and dispatched personnel from the Klang Selatan Fire and Rescue Station to the scene. "The fire involved a double-storey terraced house and caused damage to about 70 per cent of the structure.

items.title: Tengku Zafrul eyes Selangor seat in GE16, points to Pandan vacancy and Ampang groundwork
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — Senior Political Adviser to the Prime Minister, Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz, has proposed that he be given the opportunity to contest a seat in Selangor in the 16th General Election (GE16). He said the proposal had been submitted to the PKR leadership for consideration and approval by the party’s president, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Previously, former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli announced his departure from PKR and vacated the Pandan parliamentary seat.

items.title: Maybank Islamic named Global Islamic Bank of the Year 2026 by The Banker
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Islamic Bhd has been named The Banker's Islamic Bank of the Year 2026, beating competition from financial institutions across the Middle East and Asia. The bank also maintained about 30 per cent of Malaysia's Islamic banking asset market share. Maybank Group Islamic Banking also mobilised RM34.54 billion in sustainable Islamic finance across Malaysia, Singapore and Indonesia in 2025, representing about 60 per cent of Maybank Group's overall sustainable finance.

items.title: Growing Rohingya presence raises concern in Perlis
items.summary: ARAU: The rising number of Rohingya refugees in parts of Perlis has raised concern among residents, with a state assemblyman warning against complacency. A resident who identified himself only as Ali Abu, 65, said locals were worried by the growing presence of Rohingya in the state. He said concerns intensified after an estimated 300 Rohingya gathered during the recent Aidiladha celebrations.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia opens lower on profit-taking
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank fell four sen to RM11.12, Public Bank slid 13 sen to RM4.87, Tenaga Nasional rose six sen to RM14.50, CIMB decreased eight sen to RM7.70 and IHH Healthcare slid three sen to RM8.74. Among active stocks, Dagang Nexchange added 2.5 sen to 41 sen, Tanco was up half a sen to 15 sen, and Top Glove increased one sen to 73 sen. Sum Technology inched down 1.5 sen to 51 sen, while Borneo Oil was flat at half a sen.

items.title: Treat us unfairly and developing nations will deal elsewhere, Anwar tells Europe
items.summary: He said Malaysia had paid 95 per cent of the contract value of the defence-related procurement but was given no satisfactory explanation for Norway’s decision despite repeated efforts to seek clarification. The episode would influence Malaysia’s future decisions, Anwar said. Anwar said Malaysia had initially suspected the issue could have been linked to Nato and assistance from the United States.

items.title: Retired lecturer dies after car crashes into wall
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A 68-year-old retired public university lecturer died after losing control of his car, which skidded and crashed into a wall at Jalan Pahlawan yesterday. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said police received a call from the public regarding the single-vehicle accident at 4.05pm. Initial investigations revealed that the victim was driving his Proton Wira from Jalan Lagenda Utama towards Jalan Badlishah.

items.title: Police arrest 58 in illegal World Cup betting crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 19 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) have arrested 58 individuals suspected of involvement in unlicensed sports betting and online gambling syndicates in connection with the Fifa World Cup 2026. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said all suspects were detained under Op Soga XI, a nationwide operation launched on June 11 and ongoing until July 19. “Police also seized RM25,684.65 in cash and uncovered betting credit values totalling RM488,582.47,” he said in a statement here yesterday.

items.title: Police corporal charged with possessing meth in Kota Bharu court lock-up, claims trial
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 19 — A police corporal pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here yesterday to possessing 19.8 grammes of methamphetamine in the lock-up of the Kota Bharu Court Complex last week. Mohammad Fadhirul Musa, 38, made the plea before Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid. He was charged with possessing 19.8 grammes gross of methamphetamine at the lock-up of the court complex at 8.29am on June 8.

items.title: Six rescued, three injured in early morning Melaka house fire
items.summary: MELAKA: An elderly man and a woman suffered burns, while another man experienced breathing difficulties after they were trapped in a fire that destroyed a double-storey rented house in Taman Perkota Hang Tuah, Bukit Baru, early today. State Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Mohd Shahrom Laji said the department received a distress call at 3.38am. Fifteen personnel from the Ayer Keroh Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by the Bukit Katil Fire and Rescue Station, were deployed to the scene.

items.title: PM Anwar: Russia to guarantee long-term petrol, oil and gas supplies to Malaysia
items.summary: KAZAN, June 19 — Russia has agreed to guarantee long-term supplies of petrol, oil and gas to Malaysia, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said Russia had not only assured supplies of petrol, oil, and gas to Malaysia but also agreed to provide them over the long term. On Malaysia’s oil supply, Anwar said the government was currently not facing supply problems, although prices might be affected to some extent.

items.title: ‘Can’t tread too slowly’: Anwar says Malaysia must be bold in pursuing economic ties with Russia, other countries
items.summary: KAZAN, June 19 — Malaysia needs to be more bold and aggressive in strengthening economic relations with Russia and other countries that have the potential to benefit the nation, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said. This concerns me greatly,” he said. Anwar said one of the areas that requires attention is energy cooperation, including oil supplies from Russia.

items.title: #NSTviral: Myvi steals Lamborghini's thunder in viral toll plaza clip [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Perodua Myvi has once again driven itself into Malaysia's unofficial 'king of the road' folklore after a viral video showed the compact hatchback emitting flames from its exhaust after a toll plaza. The video, widely shared on Facebook, first showed a Lamborghini accelerating away from the toll plaza alongside other supercars, with 'afterfire' seen coming from their exhausts. The clip surfaced as the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show 2026 continues at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre, where supercars, concept cars and mobility technology are among the attractions.

items.title: Shah Alam enters new chapter of growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Shah Alam prepares for the opening of the LRT3, the city is entering a new chapter of connectivity, accessibility and economic opportunity. As one of the largest urban rejuvenation initiatives in Shah Alam, SA Sentral project brings together key assets and future developments to create a vibrant city centre that supports economic growth, investment opportunities and sustainable urban living. "The upcoming LRT3 presents a once-in-a-generation opportunity to unlock the full potential of Shah Alam's city centre," said Datuk Mohd Najib Ibrahim, SA Sentral chief executive officer (designate).

items.title: Anwar, Turkmenistan president to hold talks to boost trade, investment ties
items.summary: ASHGABAT (Turkmenistan): The second and final day of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's official visit to Turkmenistan will be filled with a series of high-level engagements in the Central Asian nation, which lies along the eastern coast of the Caspian Sea. He is scheduled to attend an official welcoming ceremony hosted by Turkmenistan President Serdar Berdimuhamedov at the Presidential Palace in the morning, followed by a one-on-one meeting. Both leaders will also witness the exchange of an MoU between the Transport Ministry and the State Service of Turkmenistan; an MoU between the Academy of Sciences Malaysia and the Academy of Sciences of Turkmenistan; and several documents in the field of oil and gas.

items.title: DAP names newcomer Chu Poh Yee as first Johor polls candidate, rest to be named by Monday
items.summary: KLUANG, June 19 — Newcomer Chu Poh Yee was announced yesterday as DAP’s first candidate under Pakatan Harapan (PH) for the upcoming Johor election, and will contest the Mengkibol state seat. DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke Siew Fook said the remaining candidates among the party’s 17 seats would be announced in stages up to Monday. Loke, who is also PH deputy chairman, indicated that DAP would not field any candidate in the hotly contested Puteri Wangsa state seat to make way for another PH component party.

items.title: US sanctions Lebanese officials, Hezbollah-linked business network
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US sanctioned two Lebanese officials and companies Thursday allegedly linked to a Hizbollah business network, accusing them of obstructing Lebanon's peace process and delaying the disarmament of the militant group, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported, citing statement from the US Treasury Department. "Hizbollah must disarm for Lebanon to achieve a secure and prosperous future," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a statement. Earlier Thursday, US President Donald Trump said he expects a "complete ceasefire on all fronts," including Lebanon, Hizbollah and Israel, following the signing of a US-Iran agreement aimed at ending the Middle East conflict.

items.title: Japan inflation unchanged in May after BOJ rate hike
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's core inflation was unchanged in May from April, government data showed Friday, days after the central bank hiked rates to a 31-year high because of the Middle East war. Excluding also energy, the reading was 1.8 per cent, down from 1.9 per cent and in line with market forecasts. Unadjusted, inflation inched up to 1.5 per cent from 1.4, also meeting forecasts.

items.title: EU to develop new tools to tackle China export flood
items.summary: BRUSSELS, (Belgium): EU leaders agreed during talks Thursday that the bloc must develop beefed-up trade defences to curb a surge of Chinese exports deemed an existential threat to European industry by Brussels — while simultaneously seeking "constructive dialogue" with Beijing. The 27-nation bloc's trade deficit in goods hit around 360 billion euros (RM1.7 trillion) last year, meaning Chinese exports sharply exceeded the EU's. During a two-hour summit dinner in Brussels, leaders chewed over how the EU could address the imbalance, and whether the bloc needed to boost its arsenal.

items.title: Stocks climb on chips, Brent gains on Iran deal doubts
items.summary: US West Texas Intermediate fell 19 cents or 0.25 per cent to finish at US$76.60 a barrel. The Nasdaq gained 1.9 per cent and led gains on Wall Street, while the Philadelphia semiconductor index rose 6.4 per cent. Intel shares jumped 10.6 per cent.

items.title: TERAJU-backed Al-Meswak Mu'min emerges as Bumiputera halal FMCG success story
items.summary: PENANG: Strategic support from TERAJU has facilitated the growth of Al-Meswak Mu'min into one of Malaysia's growing halal fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) companies, culminating in a major manufacturing expansion. The Bumiputera-owned company today marked the opening of its new manufacturing facility and launched its 25th anniversary celebration, underscoring a growth journey supported by RM15 million in strategic financing facilitated by TERAJU and Malaysian Technology Development Corporation (MTDC). The expansion reflects TERAJU's broader strategy of identifying and scaling high-potential Bumiputera companies through targeted financing, business development interventions and capacity-building programmes aligned with the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35).

items.title: Leading through connection
items.summary: SENIOR leaders are often judged by the quality of the results achieved by their organisations. Middle-level managers occupy a unique position within an organisation. Effective leaders recognise that directives alone are insufficient.

items.title: MAG sees stronger transit traffic through Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) is seeing strong passenger demand on Europe-bound services via Kuala Lumpur driven in part by travellers from Australia and New Zealand connecting through the Malaysian capital. MAG president and group chief executive officer Captain Nasaruddin A. Bakar said Malaysia Airlines Bhd's load factor on the Kuala Lumpur-London route had exceeded 95 per cent. Nasaruddin also said Malaysia Airlines must ensure seamless network connectivity, including providing efficient transit connections between Australia and Europe through Kuala Lumpur with transit times of around three hours.

items.title: MAG to decide on A350 replacement plans by year-end
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) expects to make a decision by year-end on its future widebody fleet, including replacement plans for Malaysia Airlines Bhd's Airbus A350 aircraft. Meanwhile, MAG's new aircraft delivery programme remains on track with the group expecting to receive 11 aircraft this year comprising of both Boeing B737 Max 8 and Airbus A330neo (new engine option). Nasaruddin said MAG has already taken delivery of six aircraft so far this year, comprising two A330neos and four B737 Max aircraft.

items.title: May trade signals more upside for Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade may have remained on a strong footing in May, reinforcing expectations that the country's record-breaking momentum could extend into the coming months despite global uncertainties. The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry said early indications pointed to another positive month ahead of the official release of May trade data by the Statistics Department today (June 19). Imports increased 20 per cent to RM153.99 billion, resulting in a trade surplus of RM28.75 billion, extending Malaysia's streak of trade surplus to 72 consecutive months since May 2020.

items.title: UN demands RSF halt 'imminent offensive' in Sudan
items.summary: GENEVA: The United Nations on Thursday called on RSF paramilitaries to halt their "imminent offensive" on the strategic Sudanese city of El-Obeid, warning of catastrophic consequences for civilians. "Stop this madness," said UN rights chief Volker Turk, following reports of a significant troop build-up by the Rapid Support Forces and allied troops around El-Obeid, as well as intensified drone strikes and artillery shelling. Turk said an imminent offensive risked serious crimes being committed, as well as deepening the catastrophic impact on the civilian population.

items.title: Paris awards honorary citizenship to Palestinian civilians, journalists
items.summary: PARIS, France: The City of Paris on Thursday granted honorary citizenship to Palestinian civilians and journalists, in a symbolic gesture of support for "the suffering the Palestinian people." We are extending a hand to an entire people," Emmanuel Gregoire, the capital's Socialist mayor, said before the Council of Paris. "Recognising the suffering of the Palestinian people in no way erases that of the Israeli people," said Gregoire.

items.title: Bandar Utama to Johan Setia: A guide to the new LRT3 Shah Alam Line
items.summary: = The Shah Alam Line comprises 20 operational stations across Petaling Jaya, Shah Alam and Klang, with four additional provisional stations reserved for future development. Among the key stops are Bandar Utama, Glenmarie 2, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato' Menteri, UiTM Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia. The LRT3 Shah Alam Line runs 37.8km from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia.

items.title: Husband of minister Nancy Shukri dies at age 65
items.summary: KUCHING, June 18 — Datuk Kamil Misuari, the husband of Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri, died at a hospital in Kuala Lumpur this evening. The Office of the Women, Family and Community Development Minister, in a statement today, said his remains would be flown back to Kuching tomorrow. “His remains will be placed at Datuk Seri Nancy’s official residence at Taman Serira, Jalan Menggeris, Petra Jaya, to allow members of the public to pay their last respects before the burial at the Samariang Muslim Cemetery.

items.title: GRS aims to contest all 25 Sabah parliamentary seats in coming general election
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 26 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) has set its sights on contesting all 25 parliamentary seats in Sabah as well as the federal territory of Labuan in the next general election, as the state’s ruling coalition begins preparations. GRS chairman Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the coalition had begun strengthening its election machinery and appointed senior leaders to oversee preparations in six electoral zones covering all parliamentary constituencies in Sabah and Labuan. GRS currently comprises Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Parti Solidariti Tanah Airku, Parti Cinta Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah and newly admitted component party Upko.

items.title: Upko joins GRS, expanding Sabah ruling coalition to six parties
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 18 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) today officially accepted the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) as its newest component party, strengthening the local coalition’s ranks ahead of the next state election. Upko becomes the sixth component party in GRS after formally applying to join the coalition last month. Hajiji, who is also Sabah chief minister and Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah president, said Upko’s admission would further strengthen the stability of the state government led by GRS.

items.title: ‘No child left behind’: SIS Forum to draft law, push new agency to make ex-husbands pay their dues
items.summary: ARA DAMANSARA, June 18 — Advocacy group SIS Forum (Malaysia) said it plans to draft its own proposed Bill to ensure all Malaysians pay the financial support owed to their spouse, former spouse and children, amid statistics on a persistent problem of men failing to pay up when marriages break down. SIS Forum also wants the Malaysian government to set up a new government agency to help all Malaysians get the financial support that should be paid by their former spouse, saying that this proposed agency should help both Muslims and non-Muslims. SIS Forum executive director Rozana Isa said the non-governmental organisation is planning to also come up with cost estimates when it prepares a draft of the suggested new law for the government.

items.title: Malaysia jumps eight spots to 15th in IMD World Competitiveness Ranking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Malaysia has jumped eight spots in the 2026 International Institute for Management Development (IMD) World Competitiveness Ranking, rising to the 15th spot among 70 economies, marking its best ranking in recent years. Last year, Malaysia advanced 11 places to 23rd among 69 economies, up from the 34th place out of 67 economies in 2024. The ranking was based on four pillars, including the country’s economic performance, government efficiency, business efficiency and infrastructure.

items.title: Court cuts Lim Guan Eng’s defamation damages against Muhyiddin from RM1.05m to RM450,000
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 18 — The Court of Appeal here today reduced the general damages awarded to DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng from RM1.05 million to RM450,000 in a defamation suit filed against Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin relating to the revocation of tax exemption for Yayasan Albukhary. A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar made the ruling after allowing part of Muhyiddin’s appeal against the Kuala Lumpur High Court’s decision. The panel also set aside the High Court’s decision in awarding RM150,000 for aggravated damages and RM150,000 for exemplary damages to Lim.

items.title: Sabah names Zainudin Aman as new state secretary
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 18 — The Sabah government has approved the appointment of Datuk Zainudin Aman as the new State Secretary, effective June 29. Zainudin, who is currently the Sabah State Public Service director-general, will succeed Datuk Seri Safar Untong, whose tenure ends on June 28. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the appointment was expected to further strengthen the Sabah civil service and enhance its competitiveness in driving the state’s administrative transformation agenda.

items.title: Higher fines, tougher crackdown on ‘mat rempit’ and ‘tonto’ under Road Transport Act amendment to be tabled next week, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) will be tabled at the Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament next week. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the proposed amendments cover 11 scopes involving 42 clauses, including the introduction of penalties for illegal racing and “tonto” activities, as well as higher fines for serious offences, Harian Metro reported today. Loke said one of the key focuses of the amendments is strengthening the digital transformation of the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to reduce reliance on conventional enforcement methods.

items.title: Johor DoE detects air pollution linked to suspected illegal oil dumping at Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG, June 18 — The Johor Department of Environment (DoE) has detected air pollution that was believed to be caused by oil residue following an inspection in Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort here. The DoE team, from the Pasir Gudang branch, were alerted on the matter after worried resident detected a chemical odour since last Saturday. Johor DoE director Mohd Famey Yusoff said the department’s enforcement team and the Taman Pasir Putih police were despatched to the affected area following the complaints.

items.title: Deputy minister: Workplace and family-linked cases dominate sexual harassment reports, 388 logged from Jan to May 2026
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, June 18 — A total of 388 sexual harassment cases were recorded during the first five months of this year, according to Deputy Minister of Women, Family and Community Development Lim Hui Ying. She said statistics from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) also showed a rising trend in reported sexual harassment cases, increasing from 477 cases in 2022 to 1,038 cases last year. “I urge colleagues, employers and family members to provide support to victims.

items.title: Labuan rescue teams recover missing eight-year-old’s body from Sungai Ganggarak Permai, 50m from where slippers dropped
items.summary: LABUAN, June 18 — The body of an eight-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Ganggarak Permai here yesterday evening was found today. He was found by search-and-rescue members comprising personnel from rescue agencies and local villagers involved in the search operation. Labuan Fire and Rescue Department director Abdul Rahman Ali said the department received an emergency call at 6.36pm yesterday and immediately launched a search-and-rescue operation.

items.title: No more warnings: Selangor JPJ to haul vehicle owners to court over rentals to foreigners in the state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ) has warned that local vehicle owners found renting out their vehicles to foreigners in the state will no longer be given warnings and will instead be taken straight to court. Selangor JPJ deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said motorcycles were among the most commonly rented vehicles, with daily, weekly and monthly arrangements offered at set rates, according to a report published by Kosmo! He said foreigners typically use the rented vehicles for business activities such as selling food, transporting gas cylinders and scrap metal.

items.title: Bersatu fails bid to overturn MACC freeze on two bank accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The High Court has dismissed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s (Bersatu) judicial review application to challenge a freeze order imposed by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on two of its bank accounts. The Star reported that Justice Aliza Sulaiman, now a Court of Appeal judge, ruled that the applicants failed to prove the MACC’s actions were ultra vires, illegal, procedurally improper, irrational or made in bad faith. Berita Harian in a separate report said that the High Court had earlier on October 30, 2023 granted Bersatu leave to initiate judicial review proceedings against MACC’s seizure of its bank accounts.

items.title: Johor police warn against 3R provocation and disrupting the peace ahead of state election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — The police will not compromise with any acts that provoke, threaten and incite hatred, including those linked to 3R issue (religion, race and royal institutions), for the upcoming state election. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said this includes acts of damaging public property, spreading false information or committing any offence that could affect public order throughout the election period. Present was Johor deputy police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat and senior public order officers.

items.title: Johor rolls out economic plan targeting RM167.4b in investments
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — The state government launched the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP) by identifying 83 investment projects across eight priority industries, which are expected to contribute investments worth RM167.4 billion. He said focusing on these industries would strengthen the state’s investment narrative, thereby giving investors confidence in the government’s consistent support. Also present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, the Economic and Investment Advisor of Johor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad, and President and Chairman of Pemandu Associates Datuk Seri Idris Jala.

items.title: Carey Island port project on track, transport minister says amid land talks with Selangor govt
items.summary: PORT KLANG, June 18 — The Selangor State Government is finalising the settlement of the land issue as the initial phase of the development of the Port Klang Third Terminal on Carey Island, here. “(The construction of this third port) will involve the Build-Operate-Transfer model. On May 11, Loke was reported to have said that the government had approved the development project of the Third Terminal of Port Klang on Carey Island in Selangor.

items.title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim vows to strengthen agency despite steep learning curve
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 18 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman has pledged to drive improvements across the agency, acknowledging that his first month in office has presented challenges due to his different professional background. Abdul Halim said every individual entrusted with leadership responsibilities must be prepared to embrace challenges and fulfil their duties without hesitation. On April 25, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, consented to Abdul Halim’s appointment as the new MACC Chief Commissioner.

items.title: RMAF strengthens South China Sea surveillance with drones and new patrol aircraft
items.summary: SUBANG, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) is progressively strengthening its Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities to enhance monitoring of the country’s maritime zones, particularly in the South China Sea. Chief of Air Force, General Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris, said the second phase of the initiative will involve procuring three additional ANKA Medium Altitude Long Endurance-Unmanned Aerial System (MALE-UAS) units. Muhamad Norazlan said the RMAF is scheduled to receive its first P-72M maritime patrol aircraft (MPA) from Italy next April, further strengthening air surveillance operations.

items.title: Miri police arrest man over string of five shop break-ins
items.summary: MIRI, June 18 — Police have arrested a 30‑year‑old local man in connection with a string of five break‑ins at business premises in the city. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the district’s Criminal Investigation Division (CID) received several reports of break-ins between mid-May and early June. With the arrest, Mohd Farhan said the district police have resolved all five cases sharing the same modus operandi, reported at Airport Commercial Centre, Assar Commercial Miri Bypass, Mosjaya, Permyjaya and Boulevard Miri, with total losses estimated at RM15,000.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin: Wawasan RoS status not an issue as party only changed name after takeover
items.summary: Hamzah said the faction he leads had taken over an existing registered party and simply changed its name. Hamzah said MPs present agreed on the need to go to the ground and support candidates as part of the election preparations. Earlier, Hamzah and 16 other MPs from Gerakan Reset Malaysia attended the meeting with PAS MPs, which lasted nearly two hours.

items.title: Grandkids’ birth certificate numbers deliver RM11.8m Toto jackpot to Perak retiree
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A 62-year-old retired salesman from Perak has won a Sports Toto 4D Jackpot 1 prize worth RM11.8 million after using numbers derived from his grandchildren’s birth certificates. The winner collected RM11,806,469.85 from the total RM12 million jackpot on May 31 after his winning numbers, 1660 and 5093, matched the jackpot combination, according to The Star . The retiree said the numbers were inspired by his two grandchildren’s birth certificate details.
